source_title: Smart home energy management systems in internet of things networks for green cities demands and services
article_summary: The article reviews IoT-based smart home energy management systems and discusses architectures and services that support energy-efficient operation in the context of green cities. It highlights techniques such as demand-side management, load scheduling, device coordination, and digital-twin or monitoring approaches that enable optimized appliance operation and shifting loads to greener/off-peak times. The paper also addresses user-facing aspects like energy-awareness and services that can promote sustainable behavior, as well as integration challenges for occupancy detection and automated control strategies that preserve comfort and safety.
